channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. — 154 replies — 262.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low.

Open thread • Replies: 154 • Updated: 2026-06-07 21:58 UTC

OP

At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. Logically thinking there would be at least SOME buying pressure from other people in the know and price would be held up at key points with traces of bullish absorption in the chart yet theres none of that just a fast move up into a slow bleed. If you do any type of chart watching you would know exactly what im talking about. With just a glance at the LINK chart you can tell there is not a single good/bullish thing about it.

You can't convince me people involved in these announcements with chainlink wouldn't buy some themselves and talk to their other high-class or wealthy friends and those people would buy and word would continue to spread. It just doesnt make sense unless.... the people with inside knowledge know LINK isnt necessary for the services chainlink provides.

Call me a faggot call me indian call me fudder call me retard but it just doesnt make ANY sense despite all of these announcements and the apparent fact that these giant companies are using chainlink that the LINK does not trend upward.

I dont know why im posting this I guess I just wanted to get some thoughts from you guys or rant into the void. Ive been in since 2021 and bought around 15 dollars. I'm not sure if I can hold much longer and on the other hand I dont know what I would even buy even if I sold. I wish I could invest in the company Chainlink.

Early replies (first 3)

62312631
How big is your stack

62312653
>LINK starts to moon in sats
>fuddies get riled up
Like clockwork

62312685
>>62312653
ironically this is exactly how they get away with their accumulation. either during btc dips or OTC buys. meanwhile retail looks at the price, demoralization continues, they continue to accumulate.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62324495
I'm new to this whole "investing" thing but isn't holding an asset that's down 70% over 5 years, like, a really bad investment? Or is it good that it goes down because you can make more money losing money like those Hollywood studios do? Just seems weird to still like and hold a thing so much that seemingly only brings you pain and suffering.

62324507
>>62314036
Why would CLL want the token to be dirt cheap when they are the primary holders and benefactors. Why a slow bleed when they could have a massive run up and dump eventually?

62324652
>>62324507
they don't need the token to appreciate in value, why? because the private deals they make bring em steady revenu that is independent of the link price, because they subsidize the network using link tokens, that are preminted, lets say to run the network for a wholz ass year it costs 50k link in fees, they get paid their 300M$ of scale money no matter how much the network fees are, they dont care they dont buy the link, in essence they are basically indirectly selling their link for cash through private deals and way higher than market price

62324749
>this board is still composed of linkie cuckolds in the big 2026.
Why?

62324777
>>62324495
generally speaking, yes. But you don't go by rule of thumbs if you're looking for those once in a lifetime golden egg investments. Chainlink is a special case in that they're the only crypto company banks are integrating their technologies with.

https://www.dtcc.com/news/2026/may/12/dtcc-collaborates-with-chainlink-to-advance-24-7-collateral-management

62324797
>>62324749
Why? Because 4chan is a safe place for all kinds of losers. (I love you all)

62324959
>>62323184
lol if I was CLG I wouldn't have to post here I'd be retired because that faggot grifted his way to where he is. The irony though of you always assuming everyone is your twitter e-celebs (the verbose xrp shill does the same with fishy iirc), meanwhile you literally post here every day, same prose, same twitter screencaps, often quoting your own twitter account. Its just bizarre.

62325222
>>62324777
checked! thanks for posting the dtcc link.
https://x.com/TomZschach/status/2062907080931946674
https://x.com/SmartContract/status/2062670460412625036
big banks including jp morgan announced they want to launch a tokenized deposit network next year also.
Means DTCC which settles stocks AND the big banks need Chainlink

62325301
>>62325222
>>62324777
nice, very nice
But yes, while you can point to 5 years down 70% and be pissy, you can also say, for something with solid fundamentals, its the perfect time to buy. All a question of if crypto is legitimate or remains a scam casino.

62327001
>>62322856
I don't have to prove that at all, you specifically said the mechanism whereby token from the uncirculating supply are used to pay the nodes "decouples the link price from the usage of the network".

62327952
>>62327001
I specifically said CLL accepting payments outside of the Link token and then paying node operators with their team wallets instead of making customers pay in Link or immediately converting the payments to Link from the market decouples the usage of the network from the price.

You are trying to make the BTC mining comparison but it falls apart. You can say CLL paying from team wallets is the same as miners mining BTC, but miners decide on the rate of BTC being mined via the difficulty and the reward per block. Node operators do not decide their rates of payment, CLL does. Miners will charge to maintain operations and so BTC's price is intrinsically tied to the cost of operations. After all, no new BTC enters the market outside of miners thus the BTC's price tag will be influenced by operation costs. >>62322856's etherscan wallet proves new Link that enters the market does not go through node operators first meaning that they're stripped of influencing the price from operational costs. This is what I mean by the Link token being decoupled from the network.

Are you deliberately being obtuse? You're not even addressing my points or logic.

62327991
>>62324959
>the team of paid KOLs whom use 4chan lingo and memes do not post on /biz/ guise!!!!
yeah, sure lil buddy

62328404
test

62328534
>>62327952
>I specifically said CLL accepting payments outside of the Link token and then paying node operators with their team wallets instead of making customers pay in Link or immediately converting the payments to Link from the market decouples the usage of the network from the price.
That's what I said you said.

>miners decide on the rate of BTC being mined via the difficulty and the reward per block
That's completely false.

You're clueless. A clueless ape.

62328546
>>62328404
Testing 1…2…3

62328573
>>62324495
Everything and everyone is going to be flushed in the crypto world and traditional world. And then the new gay system sprouts up from Chainlink. There’s going to be a lot of people out there who made it in other cryptos who then decided to invest their shit like NPC’s into index funds or whatever getting muuh 4% return and are going to be wiped out. The 4% stakers will get isn’t anything remarkable on its face compared to anything else out there except it’s going to be the baseline of the new financial system.

62328670
>>62327991
Maybe he still does, I don't know, I'm saying he's a faggot grifter, other poster (who is obviously adem) was accusing me of being him. Unless you're also him just changing your id, which wouldn't surprise me on this dead board.

62329193
>>62312690
I Sold it all for BTC, I staked some recently, didnt believe in staking before because of wrapping and bridge but with TBV that's can be bypassed. I'm all out of LINK, I hold for years but nothing to show for

62329208
>>62329193
same

62330274
>>62328534
>That's what I said you said.
Devil's in the details; not the same thing and it's why your BTC comparison doesn't work.

>>>miners decide on the rate of BTC being mined via the difficulty and the reward per block
>That's completely false.
Yes anon, there is a consensus for the reward per block. The miners are included in this consensus.

62331087
>>62330274
>not the same thing
It's the same thing.

>there is a consensus for the reward per block. The miners are included in this consensus.
You went from "miners decide" to "miners are part of the decision".

The reality is miners can't decide shit. Even if all miners are 100% in consensus among themselves, all they can do is ask the market to also agree with them. Which is something anyone with a twitter account can do.

62331850
>>62312653
LINK holders are the liberals of crypto, you're completely delusional.
You're on your way out though.

62332143
>>62331087
if miners stop the difficulty plummets
if miners want to support a fork they can redirect all the hashrate
are you legitimately retarded?

62332425
>>62332143
>if miners stop the difficulty plummets
Yes.
And the lower the lower the mining difficulty, the more smaller mining rigs can mine.

>if miners want to support a fork they can redirect all the hashrate
Yes, they'll redirect all their hashrate to a fork nobody will use.

You changed IDs for this? You fucking goofball.

62332438
All of you need to KYS’sselves

62332450
>>62332425
>miners can't decide shit
>eerrr they can but they won't
I accept your concession, keep moving the goalposts you fucking imbecile

62332473
>>62332450
>>eerrr they can but they won't

kek what the fuck are you talking about?

62332491
glad the chainlink general thread format is finding acceptance

62332578
>>62332491
You keep saying this, but it's pretty much just fuddies creating about 20 fudthreads and then bumping one ad nauseum. The amount of link fud threads on the board doesn't decrease because of this "general" at all. Maybe mods should just ban all LINK fud threads and corral them into a single fuddie general, it's beyond ridiculous now that the board is so slow.

62333330
>>62325222
Chainlink confidential compute for private tokenization too

item.title: Unstaking my LINK in ~5 hours or so. I have a bit over 8,500 staked between my wallet and my dad's mini wallet, I hope o… — 23 replies — 92.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Unstaking my LINK in ~5 hours or so. I have a bit over 8,500 staked between my wallet and my dad's mini wallet, I hope o…

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-06-07 22:22 UTC

OP

Unstaking my LINK in ~5 hours or so. I have a bit over 8,500 staked between my wallet and my dad's mini wallet, I hope one of you anons gets my spot!

Early replies (first 3)

62333114
>>62332979
Congrats. You'll be able to buy back triple your stack when it goes to $2

62333117
>>62332979
Aaaaaa anon u buddy basterd, I am travelling and won't get in

62333146
>>62332979
can u partially unstake?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62333160
>>62332979
Fuck you, I hope you miss the moon mission.

62333181
another linkie successfully deprogrammed by us FUDchads
we truly are doing the Lord's work

62333189
>>62332979
>my wallet and my dad's mini wallet
Is this you?

62333198
Ooooooohhhh stinkies!

62333209
>>62333146
I think so? But I'm unstaking it all.

62333217
>>62332979
this guy might be the last one to sell before swift/dtcc go live and thus becoming the greatest meme of the year

good job retard

62333234
>>62333217
youve had nine years nigger

62333250
>>62333217
There's always another carrot anon.
>you can't sell the next promised thing is happening!!!

62333267
>>62333217
I've seen this type of pathetic attempt at sell shaming a lot over the past few years and each and every time without fail the capitulating anon who sold always made the right choice in hindsight.

There was one at $18
Couple at $14
Last week there was one at $9 and it was right before the Bitty nuke
The one I remember the most vividly was when it was a literal troon selling LINK last cycle at the top to pay for his bottom surgery

62333271
>>62332979
>Unstaking my LINK in ~5 hours or so
k, can you give us a 1 min warning so we can outstake sdl's nigger bot

62333274
>>62333217
nobody is falling for it stinkbat

62333283
>>62332979
I'll need tighter warning to get the VPN on and everything... Godspeed OP. Thanks for the fish.

Hopefully the CAPTCHA doesn't go all "no verification needed" and break.

62333288
>>62333217
Cope and seethe. My only regret is leaving a suicide stack on my hard wallet

62333297
>>62333271
And it takes 30 days I’m pretty sure for the pool to actually open up and him get his tokens back.

62333303
>>62333297
Anon, did you not look at my screenshot? I have waited the 30 days. I have a few hours left.

62333306
>>62333297
Disregard just saw the cooldown

62333312
>>62333288
Pocket change sells

62333345
wait
>file 177
>not a screenie
>he saved pic from another thread
>another larp
>awww

62333369
>>62333345
>>he saved pic from another thread
>anon is so stupid he can't see timestamps on posts
Are all LINK cultists this retarded?

62333482
>>62332979
Wait linkcucks stake for only a 4% return? Seriously??

item.title: 6 DOLLARS — 21 replies — 84.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
6 DOLLARS

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-06-07 22:28 UTC

OP

KEK BAGGIES

Early replies (first 3)

62327248
>>62327243
It just gets worse and worse for them.

crypto a shit.

62327252
I love the slow realization of all these bagholders that their staking and holding is just going to pay the salaries of chainlink labs as they sell off the token lol, they're so shameless about it.

there is no incentive for it to go up, retards. the fair value is probably sub $5 to operate oracles, it has no reason to go up.

62327413
Let's HECKIN GO
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62329419
>>62327243
it's like a gift from Sergey at these prices

62331703
>>62327243
I trust Sergey.
I trust the white paper.
I trust the plan.
Never selling.
1k eoy.
SN=SN.

62331710
SERIOUSLY, I DON'T FUCKING GET IT.

LOOK AT FUCKING ZEC. IT HAS PUMPED $120 AFTER BEING EXPOSED. IT IS SO SHIT THAT THERE MIGHT BE AN UNLIMITED SUPPLY. AND NOBODY CARES. IT CRASHED BUT IT'S ALREADY PUMPING.

FUCKING WHY

LINK IS THE MOST FUNDAMENTAL CRYPTO IN THE ENTIRE SPACE. NO DEFI WITHOUT LINK


FUCK EVERYTHING JESUSCHRIST GOD

62331781
>>62331710
Basically TPTB aren't going to let us make it no matter what.

62331824
>>62331781
They do, but it's an IQ test, looks like you didn't pass muster

62331846
>>62331824
They don't, the stories of people making it are lies that are told to keep people motivated and on the treadmill.

62331855
>>62331781
But why can't we pump? It's madness. Even coins that have been exposed don't even crash that hard and then they keep pumping. It's pure madness. There might be an unlimited supply of ZEC that could have been used to make some insiders billionnaires. And it not only crashes to the ground, but after a crash that lasted hours, it begins to pump. ZEC is pure crap. Without LINK, defi wouldn't even exist. It's disgusting what is happening.

62331859
>>62331855
Everything is fake and gay.
EVERYTHING

62331864
>>62331855
honestly what you've said makes the chainlink thing so much worse. you're right, zioncash even after the literal confession of printed out of nowhere coins -> it STILL does a pump after.

Chainlink just continues dying for 6+ years.

62331873
>>62331846
if that helps you sleep at night, kek

62332520
how much link should I buy to make it

62332558
>>62332520
10k link tokens to be the future top 1%,
like having several billion USD now.
but you can get rich by holding 20-100 tokens, even one full token will be impressive in the future

62332565
Holding link is a fucking nightmare, holy shit.

62332823
>>62327243
I want $3 chainlink. I’ll buy 80k links at this price and then retire when chainlink goes $50 again

62332849
>>62331710
What value does Chainlink offer to society?

62332960
>>62332520
100k is make it stack

ignore all the faggots that tell you 1k or 10k link is enough

none of them made it with link in almost a decade. at this point ,

1/10 of a fucking century

62333015
>>62327243
>$7
wtf is this shit thread
you got my hopes up

62333505
Reminder that I will only start slurping when it hits $.15 and not a single cent more. You’re welcome.

item.title: Crypto used to be fun... — 11 replies — 43.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Crypto used to be fun...

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-06-07 22:18 UTC

OP

Even in 2022-2023, there was still some life left in it. New narratives were still popping up and people were hopeful. The memes were still good. The mini alt run in 2024 was nice but even I knew that it was basically over given how much of a flop coinbase's onchain summer even was. It just continued progressively getting worse with all the scams that were going on and how pvp it became. The base chain itself was rigged with deep insider trading. Even if BTC breaks a new ATH, alts will never see the gains they used to. I honestly don't see the space ever going back to what it used to be, not even close.

Early replies (first 3)

62331836
AI took all the capital that normally flows into crypto. Guess the doggy coin meme mania killed crypto for good so no investors took it seriously. Also the web 3.0 meme was the perfect mid-wit trap to capture baggies.

62331860
>>62331829
the thesis behind crypto is non existent, there is no real world demand for a cryptocoin that will prevent it from going to $0

62331901
>>62331829
Only your second bear market, Anon?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62331942
>>62331829
Wait till OP learns about cycles

62331980
>>62331942
the 2025 cycle was completley cycled because of the ongoing stock market bull fueled by AI stocks. Crypto will come back eventually, but it's not worth it to wait around and cross your fingers, could be a long time.

62332020
>>62331836
Any other narratives left, or developing narratives that will help start up a new bull run?

62332034
The dumbest people on the planet were getting alts, and shitcoin in particular, this cycle. And there's literally nobody left to act as exit liquidity.

But a new sucker is born every day, so it'll recover eventually.

62332059
>>62332020
On the tech side: tokenized real world assets (the next big deal IMO), decentralized trading, prediction markets, DePin, privacy coins, agentic AI commerce (kind of a meme so far)… Plenty of narratives that lurk in the dark and can fuel another bull run.

On the investor side: millenials (the main investors in crypto) will inherit at least 50 TRILLION dollars from their boomer parents in the next 20 years. Overall the boomers, who are notoriously averse to crypto, will discharge more than 100 trillion dollars in the next decades. And the entire crypto market in only worth 2 trillion dollars so far.

62332067
>>62332059
is* only worth

Also, institutional investors coming in.

62332078
>>62331829
on short term, the pumps are still very massive and liquid, there's even more than in 2017.
on long term, the difference is that the trend for all alts seems to be to zero, but that doesn't prevent gains.

the only difference is now you have to be actively trading, the easy mode of just buying and waiting and get a x10 might be over.

it might come back if dollar dies and volatility increases.

62333458
>>62331829
I’m still bag holding projects from 2023 that are still trying to build off of old narratives. The team, and what’s left of the ‘community’ get excited over updates that mean nothing. It’s sad to see. The only reason why I still hold any of it is because they dumped 95%+ and they aren’t worth the gas fees to sell

item.title: We're in a bubble. — 21 replies — 36.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
We're in a bubble.

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-06-07 21:59 UTC

OP

>Watched CNBC live after years
>90% of ads are about AI
>Ad about buying life insurance
>Ad about selling your life insurance
>Some old ass dude selling his SpaceX IPO on his website pre-IPO date to exit scam before the stock crashes

Explain to me how we're not in a AI bubble? Yesterday everyone in the knows are exiting because they know SpaceX will crash the markets. Then other IPO's like OpenAI and Anthropic crash it even more. They want all retail exit liquidity before the stupidty dries up.

Sell before the IPO or you'll regret it.

Early replies (first 3)

62327775
>>62327768
Haha nice try I’m gonna buy even more ai

62327776
they know it, but it's about whether the retail baggies know it. it's a bit more like dot com rather than 08' (considering your picrel).

>retail exit
there it is.

62327778
>>62327768
I’m buying the spacex ipo lol BaSeD ELoN 2 Da MoON

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62327787
>>62327777
Who got dem lucky quads

62327844
>>62327768
Your consensus is were in a bubble because CNBC has life insurance commercials

The brain rot on this board is unreal

62328007
>>62327844
[text about going to school to learn to read]

62328010
Uhh you see the jobs report is like strong and stuff which means uh we should sell everything cause like everyone having a job is like bad and stuff

62328013
If the goyim is afraid and think there is a bubble that is a strong bottom signal, BUY BUY BUY, baby!

62328049
The reddit crowd seems to be convinced all the ipos are scams. I don't plan on buying any of them but I wouldn't be surprised if this is the time an ipo doesn't dump on day 1.

62328063
>>62328010
People had jobs during the early 2000's.

>>62327844
Ah yes, ignore the test of the text.

>>62328013
Do it.

>>62328049
>I don't plan on buying any of them
Pussy

62329012
>>62328049
this one will probably get pumped pretty hard because its seen as a proxy for the us economy rn. trump and elon are relatively friendly too

62329030
>>62327768
Serious talk, we know that stocks and crypto are correlated when stocks go down but not up so when stocks crash and go to say a 30-50% retrace how low will that make crypto go? Will we see $1.00 chainlink???

62331494
>>62329030
I want to see $1.00 chainlink so that guy that keeps making threads laughing at them is happy

62331845
>>62327768
>Some old ass dude selling his SpaceX IPO on his website pre-IPO date to exit scam before the stock crashes
That's wild, can you find this on YouTube? I'm interested to see what the selling point even is. Does he claim to be selling it at a discount?

62331888
>>62331845
He looks like the guy that comes to your door in fallout 4.

62332134
>>62327768

62332463
>>62328049
To me it's overinflated itself to the point of becoming a scam. SpaceX is not worth the valuation it's being given, so that's sus in its own right. Then taking the limitations down to let retail in for liquidity's sake. It just reeks.

62332692
>>62327844
Try reading again, this time with your brain on

62332734
spcx is bullshit because anyone that wanted that money would have invested in rocket lab already

62333280
>>62332463
This is the same thing everyone says about Tesla, and it’s probably true but it doesn’t seem to have any effect.

62333332
>we are totally not cooked bro just buy more Elon stockz

>>62327844

You either have no stocks or are about to become a generational bag holder.

item.title: I cashed out ETH a few days ago. Should I pivot to BTC? I have a gut feeling there'll be more bleeding before the ipo bu… — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I cashed out ETH a few days ago. Should I pivot to BTC? I have a gut feeling there'll be more bleeding before the ipo bu…

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-07 22:31 UTC

OP

I cashed out ETH a few days ago. Should I pivot to BTC? I have a gut feeling there'll be more bleeding before the ipo but I don't know if I'm just being paranoid.

Early replies (first 3)

62332449
>>62332443
I don't know, I've been bitcoin only for a while now.

62332508
>>62332443
Congrats on panic selling the bottom. Next time do the exact opposite of your gut feeling and you might win.

62332532
>>62332443
If I saw this pic on your computer I would beat the fuck out of you.

More Christians need to be violent and intolerant of such blatant disrespect.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62332581
>>62332443
Crypto is just BTC at this point. Alt season is forever over. Just DCA into BTC or go buy boomer stocks / boomer rocks.

62332676
>>62332532
turn the other cheek you posturing faggot

62332851
>>62332676
Not when it comes to disrespecting your God. That’s when you start whipping people and flipping tables over you gay faggot.

62333495
>>62332508
I can still buy in lol

62333520
>>62332532
>“Be kind and compassionate to one another, forgiving each other, just as in Christ God forgave you. – Ephesians 4:32

item.title: Why Does Bitcoin Have 6X more Value If Ethereum Powers the Crypto Economy? — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why Does Bitcoin Have 6X more Value If Ethereum Powers the Crypto Economy?

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-06-07 22:33 UTC

OP

What does BTC actually power? If Bitcoin were to go to zero tomorrow, it seems like very little in the broader economy would be directly affected.
Ethereum, on the other hand, powers a large ecosystem of applications, including DeFi protocols and stablecoins. If ETH were to collapse in value, validator rewards would become much less attractive, potentially reducing the incentive to secure the network.
Since a significant portion of DeFi and roughly $170B worth of stablecoins operate on Ethereum and Ethereum-related infrastructure, a severe and sustained decline in ETH could have much broader consequences for the crypto ecosystem than a similar decline in BTC. Am I missing something in this reasoning?

Early replies (first 3)

62333235
Maybe only 10% of anyone that knows anything about cryptocurrencies understand what Ethereum is.

62333256
>>62333235
Even after all these years?

62333335
No one cares about defi. Cyrpto has only one use and it is to provide liquidity that is proof against censorship, embargo, and currency debasement.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62333354
>>62333256
For most people crypto is the same as Bitcoin. They might know about defi, stablecoins, NFTs and memecoins but for them it's tiny enough to be irrelevant. Their marketcap combined is less than BTC marketcap so this simplification is understandable.

62333441
>>62333196
It's a mystery to me too, but I can't ignore the charts and the ETH/BTC ratio, so I just hold BTC these days

62333512
>>62333196
>Ethereum, on the other hand, powers a large ecosystem of applications
that are totally irrelevant
>including DeFi protocols
that nobody cares about
>and stablecoins
lol, lmao, even
>If ETH were to collapse in value, validator rewards would become much less attractive
and nobody would notice
>potentially reducing the incentive to secure the network
"potentially" is doing a lot of heavy lifting there, and still nobody cares.
>If Ethereum were to go to zero tomorrow very little in the broader economy would be directly or indirectly affected
ftfy.

62333527
>>62333196
because this crypto economy is irrelevant compared to what bitcoin does

item.title: LINK DOUBTERS ON SUICIDE WATCH — 5 replies — 15.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
LINK DOUBTERS ON SUICIDE WATCH

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-06-07 22:14 UTC

OP

WTFWT, NEVER DOUBT SERGEY

Early replies (first 3)

62331095
>linkoids are now hallucinating pumps
would be sad if they weren't annoying spammers deserving of contempt

62333346
>>62331034
chainlink, you just win

62333365

item.title: GLOBAL WANTS ME TO FIRE EVERYONE IN MY DEPARTMENT ARE THEY GOING TO FIRE ME TOO WHY CANT HR DO IT WHAT THE FUCK — 31 replies — 13.9/hr
item.content:encoded:

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-06-07 20:20 UTC

OP

GLOBAL WANTS ME TO FIRE EVERYONE IN MY DEPARTMENT
ARE THEY GOING TO FIRE ME TOO
WHY CANT HR DO IT WHAT THE FUCK

Early replies (first 3)

62326586
Fuck these people have mortgages to pay and have been under me for 15 years for some. Why the fuck are they doing this to me?

62326592
>gives you one last humiliation ritual before destroying your livelihood
nothin personell... wagie...

62326612
>>62326577
lol work at a corpo every 2-3 months see people go. It’s nonstop here.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62326617
>>62326577
>>62326586
sorry man, im moving to semiconductors/hardware. yeah it's in-person and I have to relocate to fucking arizona, but holy shit they do not have the same stupid layoff bullshit as the gay WFH scam jobs have (more excuse to just offshore). I am at risk of cyclical hardship but people in semiconductors do indeed get laid off less, at the same time it's harder to get a job. but it's still better paid, and requires real engineering that cant just be done by claude code.

im just gonna code for FOSS and keep it a hobby, holy shit this will never be a stable job ever again. if it's not india, they'lkl just hire coders for $40k in argentina. it's over.

62326618
>>62326577
give them bonuses instead. what are they gonna do, fire you?

62326645
>>62326577
>GLOBAL WANTS ME TO FIRE EVERYONE IN MY DEPARTMENT
>ARE THEY GOING TO FIRE ME TOO
>WHY CANT HR DO IT WHAT THE FUCK
fire everyone. when they tried to fire you, scream really loud and say " FUCK YOU I FIRED MYSELF"

62326676
>>62326577
If your bosses are indian and you are not then they are definitely planning on firing you too

62326737
>>62326676
FUCK

62327690
>>62326577
>>62326737
Refuse. And if you must, just resign, it's over. They can find someone else to pull the trigger.

62327713
>>62326586
>Mass email term them.
>Leave a paper trail
>Not your problem if they didn’t get the memo

62327722
Announce a special event, buy some pizzas and get everyone to attend, underneath the pizzas write a message that they will see after the pizzas is eaten that says they are all fired, this will give you time to leave the building before they get to it

62327736
>>62326617
>real engineering can't just be done by Claude Code
Matter of time

62327741
>>62326577
>>62326586
I would personally love firing people if I was in that position...

62327840
Leave a cake in the break room, put “Congratulations on your future opportunities” on it, let them figure it out

62327847
Have a big meeting with everyone you're firing and tell them your entire department is getting eliminated. Tell them what's really going on

62327966
>>62326676
>>62326737
This.
Straight up, don't fire anyone and if anything fire only the indians first.

62328119
>>62327713
whats up with the influx of telegram trannies posting to my fucking board?

62329996
>>62328119
Telegram? You shut your whore mouth you frog posting faggot, I was coming here when that twink moot still had mudkips on the front page.

62330002
>>62326577
Quads and I’ll chop my cock off

62330013
>>62327736
kill your brainless LLM niggerbaby

62330936
ofc they will fire you

you think they are going to leave a manager manage nothing?

dont fire them, its not your role - tell them to ask HR

start uploading their database and their source code before IT put you on the risky user monitoring list because you appear as laid off in HR connector

t. DevSecOps

62331054
>>62330936
While that would be incredibly hilarious, OP will probably want to have another job lined up and not be black listed in the industry for doing that. There’s also ways to cause way-way more internal damage than just leaking source code for some gay ass app no one uses and will get patched out or rebuilt anyway.

>t. architect

62331055
>>62326577
If you're getting fired after you fire everybody, just don't fire anybody. What are they gonna do? Fire you?

62331066
>>62326577
Give photos and home adresses of your bosses to the people you fire, "I tried I really tried"

62331104
>>62331054
> blacklisted in the industry
no such thing

62331361
>>62330002
oof

62331375
>>62326577
I hope you are happy with yourself. Just taking away peoples jobs. People who admired you will resent you.

62331434
>>62326577
>>62326645
Fire everyone, then after they all leave, fire yourself too, scorched earth, no survivors

62331518
>>62331104
lol
lmao
zozzle even

62331671
>>62331066
they wont pay severance. when my coworkers got LAID off they had to train indians for 3 months as a condition of receiving severance.

>>62330002

item.title: 65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC — 87 replies — 11.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC

Open thread • Replies: 87 • Updated: 2026-06-07 14:42 UTC

OP

>disregard dwarves
>disregard doomerfags
>if you bought the rally, hold
>if you didn't, enjoy the discount

Bitcoin is a stateless, free, private currency that draws intrinsic value. You can buy groceries with BTC, you can't buy it with rock collections or shitcoins. And ever less the fiat bullshit can buy groceries.

>but BTC is dumping to zero--

Zoom out.

Bitcoin is the future of currency and if you haven't realized yet, you are either a fool, a scammer, or a paid shill.

Buy the fucking dip and stop selling the bottom.

‐ Yours truly, the BUYROG, pmetalsbane.

Early replies (first 3)

62309508
>>62309503
proof??

62309515
This thread now is the 64k waiting room

62309535
>>62309508
I don't need proof. I made money for everyone who bought the previous dip, who were considering cashing out and start a rock collection. And this time it isn't going to be any fucking different.

ETF returns are pathetic. PM is not investment. Bonds are tanking.

Buy BTC.

Let's get rich together.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62320468
>>62309503
The city of OP

62320500
>>62320466
>inb4 BCH is not Bitcoin either, only BSV is

62320588
>>62320500
only Bit-Diamond XD
at least they changed hash afair to not compete for same ASICs (with predictable result)

62320857
>>62320358
Good. At least now the dipshits will sell the bottom.

If you think you are getting the upperhand, you are not.

It is better for BTC to get rid of the financially illiterate than it to be worth 200k but held by imbeciles trying to scam each other.

Bitcoin is a storage of value. Want proof? Zoom out the chart.

We are winning the fight against bullshit Fiat currency, dwarven suckers, and boomer indexes.

Everyone talking shit here WISHES they had bought in years ago. Sour grapes everywhere

The BUYROG remains.

>>62320391
>cope thread

I made free money to anyone who wasn't dumb enough to now sell the bottom and make this a sucker's rally.

>>62320405
Was waiting for the inevitable BTC will go to zero comment.

It is the bottom signal.

62320897
>>62309535
>I don't need proof
>BTC 5k below your prediction.

Faggot.

62320920
>>62309503
OHNONONONONONONONONONONONONO LMAOOO

62323245
Any last words, OP?

62323584
>>62323245
>Any last words, OP?

62323600
>>62323584
>OP rn

62323880
>>62323245
>>62323584
>>62323600
YOU LOST, TRANNY

It doesn't matter if Bitcoin falls to 59k, I repeat what I said: it is good riddance that the yuppies go find another con to shove down people's throats because they are overleveraged and undereducated. No one wants their store value reserve being speculated on by retards who laugh as they sell the bottom, then complain they can't make money.

Stupidity is a tax on success.

Also, keep in mind, BTC might be 6% loss in a day, SILVER and GOLD are doing WORSE. You can't compare BTC performance against stock because it is not apples to apples, BTC can only be compared with other crypto in its asset class, and other storage value investment like PMs. And guess what? It still blows all of them out of the water, and the trade volume of BTC evaluated in any desk is far greater than all of crypto and rock collections combined. BTC brockerage platforms exist by the thousands, how many dealers of PMs exist?

I will put this even simpler for bear trolls: read the Bible, flip it open to the parable of the talents, and read what is said about the one faggot who thought he was smug and clever because he neither won, nor lost the money loaned to him. This is you, this is the whole bear market.

I am screencapping this whole shit, I have blood in my eyes. I'm going laughing my way to the bank enjoying this fire sale of BTC owned by the eternal underclass of statist peasants.

BTC will be 70k by the end of the month. Hell, it will be 60k+ by the end of the day.

If I called the bottom 10% above, it is because I overestimated the intelligence of the morons that do me a favor by forgetting this asset class exists.

May they buy ETFs and go hold the bag of billionaire scammers.

I repeat:
>if you bought, hold
>if you haven't, enjoy the sale
>if you sold, we are better without you

62323927
>>62323880
>I am screencapping this whole shit, I have blood in my eyes

62323956
>>62323880

62326531
The falling knife is around $32.8k

Screencap this and thank me later.

62326539
>>62309503
Heavy bag huh?

62326550
>>62311487
ladies and gentlemen

the future of currency

62327899
>>62326531
this guy fucks

62328894
You are missing the most obvious bottom in history. And you are proud of it.

Grim.

62328962
>>62309503
are you dumb or something mate what the fuck the thing went to 58k already

62329036
>>62328962

THIS

op confirmed tard

62329055
>>62309503
intrinsic value doesn't exist
bitcoin is going to dozens of millions but for now the end of the bear cycle is not over and the bottom will likely be end of year

62329158
>>62309515
Buy the fucking dip and stake the little you can, you can never catch the bottom. If you panic sell, it will start pumping.

62329178
I hope this thread can survive another week or two, so we can laugh at OP when Bitcoin dumps to $50k

62329324
>>62329055
There is no bear cycle, it is just retards fire selling so they have free cash to buy into SpaceX IPO and end up inevitably rugged.

The BUYROG will win in the end. I hope this thread stays up until the end od the month, when BTC is at 75k and trending upwards because S&P refused to steal people's retirement to buy the latest AI scam.

62329342
>>62329324
Man, I hope you're right
It's been 8 straight months of dumping, need to see some green for a change

62329345
>>62329324
>52% drop over the last 8 months, with lower and lower bottoms over time
>"There is no bear cycle"

62329476
>>62329342
Serious, dude, just ignore these people (bear posters are just /smg/ bots and /pmg/ twofaced assholes, go tend to your garden, and come back in a few weeks.

The last time it was the same shit. The BUYROG called the bottom, they all laughed and posted bears, then the bottom was kept, and BTC rallied to near 80k before the SpaceX IPO prepping started.

We are frankly better off without these people. BTC is literally outperforming silver and 90% of crypto right now, yet they act like this is the end of crypto (it is not).

The idea of crypto is what matters, and they hate and fear this idea because they will lose, as they have lost many times before. Just zoom out the price charts.

This is not buy x sell, it is good x evil, and we are better off without retards buying into BTC so they can pump and dump.

62329478
>>62309503
/biz/ tricked me into waiting for a lower dip during the last bottom. I'm going to DCA now

62329970
>>62309503
Dunno m8 at my local groxery store they dib't accept bitcoin. Where xan you buy groceries, other than your cherrypicked example?

62331787
>>62329478
/biz/ always tells you to buy the dip. Then the dip comes, and they tell you not to buy it.

62331939
Brought to you by Epstein and Co.: Bitcoin is a stateless, free, private currency that draws intrinsic value!
